file_title: Q3 Customer Retention Analysis - Lee's Market
introduction: Today, we will delve into the customer retention patterns observed in Q3 of 2025, and compare it with the previous quarter. This analysis aims to understand our customer behavior and identify areas for strategic improvements.

subsections.heading: Overall Customer Analysis - Q2 vs Q3
subsections.content:
• In Q2, we saw a 5% increase in online sales compared to Q1
• Foot traffic in Q3 increased by 3% compared to Q2
• We introduced a new loyalty program in Q3
• Q3 saw a 4% decline in sales of non-food items compared to Q2
• There was a 2% decrease in the average basket size from Q2 to Q3
subsections.justification: This content focuses on general sales trends and new initiatives, not specific repeat customer data.

subsections.heading: Breakdown of Repeat vs New Customers - Q3
subsections.content:
• Repeat customers made up 60% of our online sales
• New customers were more likely to make purchases in-store
• 30% of new customers in Q3 came through referrals
• Repeat customers spent on average 15% more than new customers
• The majority of repeat customers shopped in-store on weekdays
subsections.justification: This content focuses on the behavior and characteristics of new vs repeat customers, not their quarterly percentages.

subsections.heading: Impact of Customer Retention on Revenue
subsections.content:
• A 5% increase in customer retention can increase profits by 25-95%
• Repeat customers spent 33% more per transaction than new customers
• Our customer retention rate in 2024 was 68%
• In 2025, we aim to increase our customer retention rate to 75%
• Our top 20% repeat customers contribute 80% of our revenue
subsections.justification: This content focuses on the impact of customer retention on revenue, not specific Q2 vs Q3 repeat customer percentages.

conclusion: Our analysis indicates a significant portion of our customer base is comprised of repeat shoppers. Moving forward, we should continue to nurture our relationships with these customers while developing strategies to convert new customers into regular shoppers.
